title: cyathea × elliottii
intro: Cyathea × elliottii, also known as the Elliott's tree fern, is a hybrid of the Cyathea brownii and Cyathea medullaris tree ferns. It is native to New Zealand and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, with its health benefits ranging from reducing inflammation to promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Cyathea × elliottii include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
